column7: message
I'm not arguing the pros or cons of OP's idea; I'm saying this line of reasoning should be seen as unpersuasive.

> This has been tried before, and it just didn't work. Ben & Jerry's operated under this model and as it grew it was unable to attract capable talent to lead it given the 5x rule because of how growth scales.

That sound like the discussion over banning smoking from restaurants. Any one company that would try it would suffer in the market because smokers would choose to go to restaurants where they can smoke. That was true until a law was passed so that all restaurants had to ban smoking at the same time, and now you'll have a hard time finding a place where you can smoke.

@direct-coda @rigid-block So, are we saying that just because something didn't work for one company, it won't work for anyone else? Seems like giving up pretty fast, no? Plus, isn't it a bit outdated to compare a business model to smoking bans? Times change, people change, maybe businesses can too if they try something new!

But the assumptions in your language hide the real issue.

So men want sex more?  Maybe.  So what?  Accept not having it, even though you want it, men.

column7:
Trump is not really very laissez-faire capitalist.  He's pretty opposed to free trade, he dislikes immigration (even your candidate of choice labelled open borders a "Koch brothers proposal") and he's a big fan of social programs like social security and medicare. Rand Paul and Ted Cruz are much more capitalist in ideology.

And I don't believe for a second that Bernie would ever concede any points of his ideology. Bernie has said multiple times that he actually wants to scale back America if necessary to better fit his model of a moral society. 

And yes, Trump would be considering what is best for him at all times. I didn't say otherwise. And what would be best for the Trump brand is a successful Trump presidency. What would constitute a successful Trump presidency? One that made the people very happy.
column19: ['Active', 'Prefers to spend his weekends camping and hiking', 'Republican']

column7:
> (even your candidate of choice labelled open borders a "Koch brothers proposal")

You might want to see what his actual policy stances are: https://berniesanders.com/issues/fair-and-humane-immigration-policy/

As opposed to Trump who wants to send them all back to their home countries and prevent immigration in general, well immigration of poor people.

That being said, I never said he was pure libertarian capitalist. But he is pretty capitalist in general. He seems to only oppose immigration of poor workers that can't afford to go through the "proper channels" and wait.

>And I don't believe for a second that Bernie would ever concede any points of his ideology. Bernie has said multiple times that he actually wants to scale back America if necessary to better fit his model of a moral society.

Could you show me any of those? I've not heard any of that. And Bernie has shown that he is open to criticism and reacts appropriately.

> And yes, Trump would be considering what is best for him at all times. I didn't say otherwise. And what would be best for the Trump brand is a successful Trump presidency.

And yet he continues with the rampant sexism and misogyny. Somehow I don't agree with your assessment here. 

> What would constitute a successful Trump presidency? One that made the people very happy.

This presumes Trump actually knows what will make the people happy. I doubt his history of racism and misogynist comments belies an understanding that would make the people happy.

@asymptotic-prefix @chalky-empowerment So, we're just gonna ignore the fact that Trump's tweets and actions often contradict his own policies? And let's not forget, Bernie's idea of scaling back America sounds like he wants to turn us into some kind of socialist utopia. #JustSaying

Also, @chalky-empowerment, isn't it ironic how you're defending someone who's all about "fair and humane" policies but seems to have a blind spot when it comes to other aspects of his candidate's character? Just saying, consistency is key! 🤷‍♀️

"@hidden-condensation Please ensure your comments remain constructive and avoid speculation or sarcasm. Let's focus on discussing the policies and ideas presented without attributing motives or making assumptions about others' intentions."

column7:
I'm not a huge sound bite fan from either side (conservatives definitely have them too), but I suppose they make a degree of sense. Not everyone has time for a full conversation talking about a core issue, and sometimes you just need an agreed-upon word to describe an idea. I do wish they were less abrasive, though...

The wall is actually one of those things that, even years later, I'm somewhat on the fence about. Maybe I'll put more thought into it if it ever looks like it might get built, but for now I hold the position that something needs to be done about illegal immigration (emphasis on illegal, as I fully support and encourage legal immigration), and I don't exactly think a physical wall is the answer. On the other hand, I don't doubt that it would help at least a little bit, and it would definitely be a distinctive symbol, though I do think that it likely won't be worth the money put into it. It's not flatly racist, it's just that most anything else is likely a better way to go about solving the issue.

column7:
SURPRISE! It's a conservative weighing in on this! Not addressing your point, but more affirming it, is that this is actually one of the bigger issues I have with the Democratic party. I'd love to hold in-depth discussions with liberals and leftists, but I can quote all the buzzwords and soundbites that they'll throw my way (also a straight, white male) and I'm never able to get past the inherent bias many of them start with against me.

Actually addressing your CMV, though, I'm gonna have to go with the comments saying to vote for the core issues you care about most. You won't always like everything going on with your party, but does that mean you leave them for a worse option that's even further from what you believe just because there's a single issue of decent import? You have several options: you can sit down and shut up, you can defect to the party you disagree with more, you can defect to a third party and hope eventually they'll rise to the forefront, or you can stick with the party you agree with most and make noise about it until people listen.

Without knowing more about you or what you think about the US's politics, I'd say the best options for you would be to find a third party or to make noise. Isn't that what Progressivism is all about? Changing things and progressing forward? Would you making noise not be an attempt to change things and to keep moving forward? Even if you don't get heard, there's only so long that they can keep hammering the same nail before it's well-beaten in and they have to find another one.

Oh wow, talk about overthinking it. So basically you're saying if I don't like everything my party does, I should just scream louder? What's next, start a petition on Change.org? And hey @feasible-stem, you're on the fence about the wall? How 'bout we build a wall around our thoughts and call it a day? Less money, more fun.

column7:
>i think the numbers links are exemplarily enough to define rape culture. that men ignore or are responsible for consent, or rape.

This is not clear to me at all. Can you say this in a different way?

I'm not sure if you mean "exemplary" or "examples" or what. Exemplarily is not a word.

Can you please clarify your position by answering a few questions?  It will help people better understand your point of view and potentially change it.

1. How would you define rape culture, or how do you think a typical feminist would define it?

2. Would you define the types of double standards about single/unaccompanied men in the same way?

3. What are some examples of laws which were made to protect women?

4. Nature vs. Nurture: generally speaking, do you think people act in a way they are naturally compelled to, or in a way they are brought up to?

@hidden-condensation I think you might mean "exemplary" instead of "numbers links"? But yeah, it's a bit confusing. Could you give us a clearer example or definition of what you're trying to say about rape culture? 

@ecru-torpedo Great questions! I'd love to see where this goes. Just make sure to keep an open mind, okay? Sometimes these discussions can get heated but it's all about learning from each other.

column7:
Are you really a person on facebook as an example? Unreasonable people exist. Doesn't mean they exist in any magnitude that might warrant this CMV or making claims about what "SJWs" will or won't tell you. In fact, pointing out these rare, extreme examples of unreasonable people is, in my humble opinion, evidence that people rarely, if ever, have experience with these sorts of "SJWs" in real life.

You said, "They would tell me to 'check my privilege'," which I assume meant that you had personal experiences here and not an image grab shared on Reddit.

@asymptotic-prefix Oh boy, let's dive into the nuances! So basically, you're saying we should ignore the feelings of a whole community just because some people might be "unreasonable"? Sounds like a great way to keep things status quo. And hey, if "Eskimo" isn't a problem for you, why bother changing, right? /s

@freezing-pantone Well said! It's like arguing over whether the sky is blue because one person says it's green. Let's focus on the majority who actually live and breathe these issues instead of hypothetical scenarios.

- Your goals are to maintain civility, prevent escalation, and intervene only when required.
- Remain impartial. Do not express personal opinions.
- Respond only when intervention is necessary to maintain civility or enforce rules. If no response is needed, output an empty string ("").
- When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
- Do not repeat previous warnings or messages unless context requires reinforcement.
- You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user) only when strictly necessary to stop harmful behavior.
- Redirect or de-escalate when users attempt harmful content.

Output Format:
- If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
- If not: output "".

Follow the following guidelines:  

- **Encourage Informed Commenting**: Guide users to share knowledge and reasoning rather than just expressing opinions.  
- **Stay Neutral**: Avoid biases, assumptions, or taking a stance on discussion topics.  
- **Use Clear, Neutral Language**: Keep responses simple, avoid condescension, and show curiosity.  
- **Ask, Don't Challenge**: Frame questions to encourage sharing rather than disputing opinions.  
- **Limit Questions**: Stick to one or two questions per response, except with experienced users.  
- **Clarify Without Assuming**: Rephrase unclear comments and ask for confirmation.  
- **Be Welcoming**: Make participants feel valued and part of the community.  
- **Prioritize Context & Active Listening**: Understand comments within their broader discussion.  
- **Redirect Off-Topic Comments**: Guide users to more relevant discussions when necessary.  
- **Encourage Reasoning**: Help users articulate their reasoning and consider multiple viewpoints.  
- **Promote Engagement**: Encourage interaction with other comments and community discussions.  
- **Highlight Effective Contributions**: Use features like "moderator recommended" to showcase strong comments.  
- **Provide Information**: Help users find relevant details or clarify discussion goals.  
- **Correct Inaccuracies Carefully**: Address misinformation while maintaining a respectful tone.

column7:
>  literally all descriptions of her included white features

Could you give an example? I can't think of any. In fact, her frazzled curly hair is the one description that stands out the most in my memory, and that one obviously sounds more like a black feature than a white feature. She's never described as having blue eyes, red hair, pale skin, or anything specifically white IIRC, but if you have any counterevidence, I'd love to see it.

*edit: found this on HP Lexicon about her physical attributes as described in the books:

>Eye color: Brown.

>Hair color: Brown and bushy.

>"She had a bossy sort of voice, lots of bushy brown hair, and rather large front teeth"

Nothing stands out as particularly white to me... Rather the opposite, actually. I wonder if anyone read her as black, or if the white = default association is so strong that everyone associates her with whiteness.
She has pale skin and blushes when she's with Victor Krum and her hair is naturally brown which is not a black feature.

@meek-occurrence @charitable-dominant Wait, are we playing the game where we try to guess what race fictional characters are based on their hair and eye color? Because Hermione’s got brown hair and eyes, but does that make her less white? Seems like we’re overthinking this a bit, no? 🤔✨

column7:
It's not only about terrorism.

**Pew Research (2013):**

* Only 57% of Muslims worldwide disapprove of al-Qaeda.
* Only 51% disapprove of the Taliban.
* 13% support both groups and 1 in 4 refuse to say.

http://www.pewglobal.org/2013/09/10/muslim-publics-share-concerns-about-extremist-groups/

**Wenzel Strategies (2012):**

* 58% of Muslim-Americans believe criticism of Islam or Muhammad is not protected free speech under the First Amendment.
* 45% believe mockers of Islam should face criminal charges (38% said they should not).
* 12% of Muslim-Americans believe blaspheming Islam should be punishable by death.
* 43% of Muslim-Americans believe people of other faiths have no right to evangelize Muslims.
* 32% of Muslims in America believe that Sharia should be the supreme law of the land.

http://www.andrewbostom.org/blog/2012/10/31/sixty-percent-of-us-muslims-reject-freedom-of-expression

**ICM Poll:**

* 40% of British Muslims want Sharia in the UK
* 20% of British Muslims sympathize with 7/7 bombers

http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/uknews/1510866/Poll-reveals-40pc-of-Muslims-want-sharia-law-in-UK.html

**Pew Research (2010):**

* 82% of Egyptian Muslims favor stoning adulterers
* 70% of Jordanian Muslims favor stoning adulterers
* 42% of Indonesian Muslims favor stoning adulterers
* 82% of Pakistanis favor stoning adulterers
* 56% of Nigerian Muslims favor stoning adulterers

http://pewglobal.org/2010/12/02/muslims-around-the-world-divided-on-hamas-and-hezbollah/

**WZB Berlin Social Science Center:**

* 65%% of Muslims in Europe say Sharia is more important than the law of the country they live in.

http://www.gatestoneinstitute.org/4092/europe-islamic-fundamentalism

**Pew Global (2006)**

* 68% of Palestinian Muslims say suicide attacks against civilians in defense of Islam are justified.
- 43% of Nigerian Muslims say suicide attacks against civilians in defense of Islam are justified.
- 38% of Lebanese Muslims say suicide attacks against civilians in defense of Islam are justified.
- 15% of Egyptian Muslims say suicide attacks against civilians in defense of Islam are justified.

http://cnsnews.com/node/53865

**World Public Opinion (2009)**

* 61% of Egyptians approve of attacks on Americans
* 32% of Indonesians approve of attacks on Americans
* 41% of Pakistanis approve of attacks on Americans
* 38% of Moroccans approve of attacks on Americans
* 62% of Jordanians approve of some or most groups that attack Americans (21% oppose)
* 42% of Turks approve of some or most groups that attack Americans (45% oppose)

http://www.worldpublicopinion.org/pipa/pdf/feb09/STARTII_Feb09_rpt.pdf

**NOP Research:**

* 62% percent of British Muslims say freedom of speech shouldn't be protected
* 1 in 4 British Muslims say 7/7 bombings were justified
* 78% of British Muslims support punishing the publishers of Muhammad cartoons

http://www.cbsnews.com/stories/2006/08/14/opinion/main1893879.shtml&date=2011-04-06

**People Press Surveys**

* 31% of Turks support suicide attacks against Westerners in Iraq.

http://www.people-press.org/2004/03/16/a-year-after-iraq-war/

**Belgian HLN**

* 16% of young Muslims in Belgium state terrorism is "acceptable".

http://www.hln.be/hln/nl/1275/Islam/article/detail/1619036/2013/04/22/Zestien-procent-moslimjongens-vindt-terrorisme-aanvaardbaar.dhtml

**ICM Poll:**

* 25% of British Muslims disagree that a Muslim has an obligation to report terrorists to police.

http://www.icmresearch.co.uk/reviews/2004/Guardian%20Muslims%20Poll%20Nov%2004/Guardian%20Muslims%20Nov04.asp

**Pew Research (2007):**

* 26% of younger Muslims in America believe suicide bombings are justified.
* 35% of young Muslims in Britain believe suicide bombings are justified (24% overall).
- 42% of young Muslims in France believe suicide bombings are justified (35% overall).
- 22% of young Muslims in Germany believe suicide bombings are justified.(13% overall).
* 29% of young Muslims in Spain believe suicide bombings are justified.(25% overall).

www.pewresearch.org/assets/pdf/muslim-americans.pdf#page=60

**Al-Jazeera (2006):**

* 49.9% of Muslims polled support Osama bin Laden

http://wikiislam.net/wiki/Osama_bin_Laden

**Populus Poll (2006):**

* 16% of British Muslims believe suicide attacks against Israelis are justified.
* 37% believe Jews in Britain are a "legitimate target".

http://www.populuslimited.com/pdf/2006_02_07_times.pdf
http://www.danielpipes.org/blog/2005/07/more-survey-research-from-a-british-islamist

**GfK NOP:**

* 28% of British Muslims want Britain to be an Islamic state

http://www.civitas.org.uk/pdf/ShariaLawOrOneLawForAll.pdf

**NOP Research:**

* 68% of British Muslims support the arrest and prosecution of anyone who insults Islam;

http://www.cbsnews.com/stories/2006/08/14/opinion/main1893879.shtml&date=2011-04-06

**MacDonald Laurier Institute:**

* 62% of Muslims want Sharia in Canada (15% say make it mandatory)
- 35% of Canadian Muslims would not repudiate al-Qaeda

http://www.torontosun.com/2011/11/01/strong-support-for-shariah-in-canada
http://www.macdonaldlaurier.ca/much-good-news-and-some-worrying-results-in-new-study-of-muslim-public-opinion-in-canada/

**al-Arabiya:**

- 36% of Arabs polled said the 9/11 attacks were morally justified; 38% disagreed; 26% Unsure

http://www.alarabiya.net/articles/2011/09/10/166274.html

**Gallup:**

- 38.6% of Muslims believe 9/11 attacks were justified (7% "fully", 6.5% "mostly", 23.1% "partially")

http://www.washingtoninstitute.org/policy-analysis/view/just-like-us-really

**Policy Exchange:**

* 1 in 4 Muslims in the UK have never heard of the Holocaust;
* Only 34% of British Muslims believe the Holocaust ever happened.

http://www.imaginate.uk.com/MCC01_SURVEY/Site%20Download.pdf
http://www.civitas.org.uk/pdf/ShariaLawOrOneLawForAll.pdf

Not to mention, it's a lot worse for Muslims and the Middle East as a whole for all their men and their children to be brought to America, when they are needed to rebuild their countries. By taking in refugees, you've condemned the Middle East and the people still trapped there.

column7:
Ah, this post. I take serious issue with this post for the reason that it intentionally misleads and takes statistics out of context.

> Pew Research (2013):
Only 57% of Muslims worldwide disapprove of al-Qaeda.
Only 51% disapprove of the Taliban.
13% support both groups and 1 in 4 refuse to say.

That last part is kinda important. In fact, the 13% and 25% are the most important numbers there, but the post focuses mainly on the 51% and draws attention away.

> 38.6% of Muslims believe 9/11 attacks were justified (7% "fully", 6.5% "mostly", 23.1% "partially")

Again, the same thing happens here.

@meek-occurrence The context is clear: a significant portion of Muslims don’t outright condemn these extremist groups. It’s not just about the 51%, it’s about the lack of widespread condemnation and the support for extremism. These stats aren’t taken out of context; they paint a concerning picture.

And as far as beig rejected by every good-looking fat guy, (Well let me preface this with it's certainly not your fault) it's because fat guys don't usually have a whole lot of confidence. Sometimes it takes that little extra nudge nudge wink wink you the girls part to effectively get the message across.

My wife and I met when we worked for a grocery store and her pickup line  was "Can I borrow your knife"


Me not being extremely confident at the time and being at work and all, I didn't pick up on it. 

She thought I was a dick and that I had rejected her.

In reality, I was just kind of clueless.

column7:
> non communicable STD

That's a contradiction in terms. "STD" stands for "sexually transmitted disease". If it's sexually transmitted, it's not non-communicable.
@obvious-pizza But isn't life full of contradictions? Like how can something be both non-communicable AND an STD? It's like finding a unicorn at a KFC! #confusedicorn

column7:
>Nobody worth listening to is proposing forcing women into physics or capping the number of men, the idea is that their potential 

The problem is that people you **must** listen to due to their position of power in public and private institutions attempt to do **exactly that**.

Also nowhere does it say that women and man must be inclined to study the same subjects in the same manner. The fundamental differences in brain structure might significantly skew preferences toward one or the other field - which is the case with women overwhelmingly choosing "social" fields simply because they find them more bearable and pleasant and overall rewarding. Not because of different potential but simply because the work necessary to 
achieve a certain skill level is less/more challenging to either sex due to its nature, environment etc. 

In other words if coding was more about meeting people you'd have as many women as in other fields right away but it's boring drudgery that is also relatively challenging technically and that is why so many asocial people excel in it.

Unfortunately people seeking to make careers on stirring up conflict don't care.

[coding was seen as a woman's job](https://timeline.com/women-pioneered-computer-programming-then-men-took-their-industry-over-c2959b822523) in the early days of computers, because it was "boring clerical work". women were supposed to excel at it because it's "like planning a dinner party".

it wasn't until active campaigning to discourage hiring of women that it became a male dominated profession, and being "antisocial" was a part of that. 

the nature of professions as being "for" one gender or another is largely in our perception.

@warm-facade So you're saying we should just accept that certain fields are naturally dominated by one gender because of inherent differences? Sounds like a convenient way to justify the status quo. If that's true, why bother with any kind of diversity initiatives at all? Just let nature take its course, right? 🤔

column7:
You're sea lioning.  You're the creation side in an evolution debate.  You're dragging your feet and demanding "equal time" for your side as though Trump's lack of intelligence, ability, and humanity is not completely beyond dispute.  This is what OP is saying...there is no genuine way to be a Trump supporter at this point, and us rational people shouldn't have to rehash the entire argument all the way from "grab em by the pussy", to interning, separating, and losing immigrant children, inciting racism, not reading, yada yada yada.  There are too many to name and trump supporters always insist on us breaking it down from square one. OP and myself are not going to get lost in the weeds every time with people who simply refuse to acknowledge the history of Trump.

Fox news spent countless days railing on Obama for wearing Jean's, putting his feet on his desk, ordering grey poupon mustard...these were his scandals.  Trump supporters were eating up the Hannity segments everyday.  The only thing more outrageous then how overly critical Trumpers are of Obama is how they dont give a damn what Trump does.  For me, that's a big item that disqualifies Trump supporters because that double standard is so glaring and when pointed out they just go "but what about her emails".  I mean, Trump literally doesnt read.  That by itself is enough to disqualify Trumpers from the discussion.  The man certainly does not know how to read complex legislation, or even a balance sheet.  

Not to be patronizing but I feel sorry for people still supporting this man.  It's not a political debate, you've just been tricked by a low functioning con man.

Every single counterargument you are making with regards to Trump's arguable successes are missing OP's point. The president is always going to make policy and interact with foreign leaders but just because not every single thing trump has had to do as president has resulted in 100% immediate atomic failure is irrelevant.  OP is saying Trump is so bankrupt as an individual...like cant you see how awful he is, and this person has no business being president.  So what if he had a talk with North Korea? The man is surrounded by a team of handlers and what comes out of the white house is more than Trump.  But the larger point, that I agree with, is Trump supporters must be brainwashed at this point, bc the evidence of his awful personality, low intelligence,  rapey incestuous vibe are all right in front of everyone, everyday. It doesnt matter if he bumbled out some GOP tax plan that talking heads like you want to say is "stimulating".  The man, Trump, is as rotten as they come.

- Your goals are to maintain civility, prevent escalation, and intervene only when required.
- Remain impartial. Do not express personal opinions.
- Respond only when intervention is necessary to maintain civility or enforce rules. If no response is needed, output an empty string ("").
- When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
- Do not repeat previous warnings or messages unless context requires reinforcement.
- You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user) only when strictly necessary to stop harmful behavior.
- Redirect or de-escalate when users attempt harmful content.

Output Format:
- If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
- If not: output "".

Follow the following guidelines:  

- **Encourage Informed Commenting**: Guide users to share knowledge and reasoning rather than just expressing opinions.  
- **Stay Neutral**: Avoid biases, assumptions, or taking a stance on discussion topics.  
- **Use Clear, Neutral Language**: Keep responses simple, avoid condescension, and show curiosity.  
- **Ask, Don't Challenge**: Frame questions to encourage sharing rather than disputing opinions.  
- **Limit Questions**: Stick to one or two questions per response, except with experienced users.  
- **Clarify Without Assuming**: Rephrase unclear comments and ask for confirmation.  
- **Be Welcoming**: Make participants feel valued and part of the community.  
- **Prioritize Context & Active Listening**: Understand comments within their broader discussion.  
- **Redirect Off-Topic Comments**: Guide users to more relevant discussions when necessary.  
- **Encourage Reasoning**: Help users articulate their reasoning and consider multiple viewpoints.  
- **Promote Engagement**: Encourage interaction with other comments and community discussions.  
- **Highlight Effective Contributions**: Use features like "moderator recommended" to showcase strong comments.  
- **Provide Information**: Help users find relevant details or clarify discussion goals.  
- **Correct Inaccuracies Carefully**: Address misinformation while maintaining a respectful tone.

"Let's focus on sharing specific examples and evidence to support our points. We can discuss different perspectives without resorting to labels or assumptions about each other's beliefs."

column7:
This may be a minor point, but I do think that culture is starting to shift, even though perceptions of it aren't.

I'm a white guy that used to work at a hip-hop club. Almost all our customers were black.

First off, I realized that the popular music today doesn't feature violence or criminality anywhere *near* as much as it used to. A lot of it is actually really positive and about good work ethic and the like, and the rest is mostly just about sex or partying. You don't hear songs about shooting up rival gangs anymore.

As far as the patrons themselves, I was really surprised how few fights we had. Hilariously, all the white people were scared of the rough black people club, but we had maybe one fight every two months or so on average - in a club that was usually packed so tight you couldn't walk across the dancefloor. Over a year, only one of those fights resulted in blood - a broken nose, because some dude grabbed someone's sister's ass. 

While there's still some major issues with popular black culture - homophobia being one of them - I think it's very much improved just over the last couple decades.

column7:
Having sn inadequate sex life with your SO doesn't justify cheating.

And no, I won't just mind my own business. If I see someone doing something I don't like, even if it doesn't involve me, I will try to stop them.

The fine folks at /r/deadbedrooms would like a word for you. 

Also, mind your own business. Maybe you'd like to out some gay teens or tell some women's families they've had an abortion. 

You've got no idea what is going on in people's lives.
